name: orchestration
description: Generic orchestration framework. The main session acts as orchestrator and dispatches work to architect, engineer, and reviewer specialists. A Phase 0 settings-manager agent deterministically detects the workspace topology (single-repo, monorepo, or a parent folder of independent repos) and profiles each member (stack, gates, and knowledge links to its CLAUDE.md/skills); the personal, gitignored workspace profile is derived from those detected facts plus your durable overrides, so re-detection never clobbers your choices and your config never gets committed. A SessionStart readiness check flags an unconfigured folder with a visible notice and then, on your next prompt, asks how to proceed (configure now / skip this session / disable here); once configured, it primes the session to route code work through /orchestrate. Both behaviors are independently toggleable (readiness_check, proactive_orchestration). Each run keeps an on-disk manifest (baselines + per-task status) so interrupted runs resume instead of restarting, and gates are independently re-run by the orchestrator rather than trusted from the engineer's self-report. Specialists are project-agnostic and learn each member's stack and conventions from its CLAUDE.md at runtime.
